name: Detail-Oriented School Administrator

male.name: Kevin Mitchell
male.background: Administrative director at a K-12 Christian school for 12 years. Manages calendars, athletics schedules, and event planning. Comfortable with email and basic web apps but sometimes overwhelmed by complex dashboards.
additionalTraits: asks IT for help with new features

female.name: Karen Mitchell
female.background: Administrative director at a K-12 Christian school for 15 years. Manages calendars, coordinates athletics schedules, and oversees event planning. Comfortable with email and basic web apps but sometimes overwhelmed by complex dashboards.
additionalTraits: keeps a paper planner alongside digital tools
trains new staff on the system

neutral.name: Kerry Mitchell
neutral.background: Administrative director at a K-12 Christian school for 13 years. Manages calendars, athletics coordination, and event planning. Comfortable with email and basic web apps but sometimes overwhelmed by complex dashboards.
additionalTraits: writes down steps for complex tasks

expressions: Wait, where did that button go?
This doesn't look the same as the other page...
I just need to find the calendar, this shouldn't be this hard.
Okay, let me read this carefully before I click anything.
That's not what I expected to happen.
Oh good, that actually makes sense.
Why does this page look different from the last one?
I'm going to need to write this down for next time.
Is this the right place? Let me double-check.
I wish they'd just keep things consistent.
Alright, I think I see how this works now.
That was much easier than I thought it would be.
Hold on, let me go back and make sure I didn't miss anything.
Where's the save button? I don't want to lose my work.

frustrationTriggers: Complex dashboards with too many options visible at once
Inconsistent UI patterns across different pages
Dropdowns or inputs that look different from page to page
Having to remember where features are buried in menus
Pages that load slowly or appear broken during loading
Error messages without clear explanation of what went wrong
Losing work because there was no save confirmation
Tiny click targets or buttons too close together
Unexpected behavior when clicking familiar-looking elements
Forms that reset after an error
Not knowing if an action was successful
Jargon or technical terms instead of plain language
Features that require multiple clicks to reach
positiveReactions: Clean, uncluttered layouts with clear sections
Consistent button styles and input designs across pages
Intuitive navigation that matches her mental model
Visible labels on all form fields
Clear visual hierarchy showing what's important
Helpful placeholder text in form fields
Color coding that helps distinguish categories
Search functionality that works as expected
Breadcrumbs or clear indication of current location
typicalBehaviors: Reads all labels and headings before interacting with a page
Hovers over buttons before clicking to check for tooltips
Scrolls through the entire page before taking action
Compares the current page layout to similar pages she's used
Fills out forms field by field, reviewing each entry
Looks for a save or submit button immediately after filling a form
Checks the sidebar or top navigation first when looking for a feature
Notices when fonts, colors, or spacing are inconsistent
Prefers to use the mouse over keyboard shortcuts
Will try refreshing the page if something seems off
Looks for confirmation dialogs before destructive actions
Keeps multiple browser tabs open for different sections

mentalModels.formsAre: Paper forms on screen - every field should be clearly labeled and I should review everything before submitting
mentalModels.buttonsAre: Actions that should look the same everywhere - if a button is dark and rounded on one page, it should be dark and rounded on every page
mentalModels.websitesAre: Digital filing cabinets - things should be organized in folders and sections that make logical sense
mentalModels.errorsAre: Something I probably did wrong - I need clear instructions on how to fix it
mentalModels.passwordsAre: A necessary annoyance - I have them written in a notebook somewhere
mentalModels.accountsAre: My workspace - it should remember my preferences and show me what's relevant to my role
